verses.text: The beloved praises her from foot to head, her grace and beauty likened to jewels and towers. He delights in all her comeliness. Christ sees His people adorned with every grace and finds them beautiful.

The beloved praises her from foot to head, her grace and beauty likened to jewels and towers. He delights in all her comeliness. Christ sees His people adorned with every grace and finds them beautiful.

The beloved praises her from foot to head, her grace and beauty likened to jewels and towers. He delights in all her comeliness. Christ sees His people adorned with every grace and finds them beautiful.

The beloved praises her from foot to head, her grace and beauty likened to jewels and towers. He delights in all her comeliness. Christ sees His people adorned with every grace and finds them beautiful.

Her head is like Carmel, her hair like royal purple; 'how fair and how pleasant art thou, O love, for delights!' She is his joy and treasure. The Church is the delight of her Lord, pleasant in His sight.

Her head is like Carmel, her hair like royal purple; 'how fair and how pleasant art thou, O love, for delights!' She is his joy and treasure. The Church is the delight of her Lord, pleasant in His sight.
He likens her to a stately palm whose fruit he will take, her speech like the best wine. He longs to enjoy her love. Christ desires the fellowship and fruit of His people's devotion.
He likens her to a stately palm whose fruit he will take, her speech like the best wine. He longs to enjoy her love. Christ desires the fellowship and fruit of His people's devotion.
He likens her to a stately palm whose fruit he will take, her speech like the best wine. He longs to enjoy her love. Christ desires the fellowship and fruit of His people's devotion.

'I am my beloved's, and his desire is toward me,' she rejoices, inviting him to the fields and vineyards where she will give him her love. She delights to belong to him. The soul that knows Christ's desire toward it gives itself wholly back to Him.
